_module.args.description:
Additional arguments passed to each module in addition to ones
like `lib`, `config`,
and `pkgs`, `modulesPath`.

This option is also available to all submodules. Submodules do not
inherit args from their parent module, nor do they provide args to
their parent module or sibling submodules. The sole exception to
this is the argument `name` which is provided by
parent modules to a submodule and contains the attribute name
the submodule is bound to, or a unique generated name if it is
not bound to an attribute.

Some arguments are already passed by default, of which the
following *cannot* be changed with this option:
- {var}`lib`: The nixpkgs library.
- {var}`config`: The results of all options after merging the values from all modules together.
- {var}`options`: The options declared in all modules.
- {var}`specialArgs`: The `specialArgs` argument passed to `evalModules`.
- All attributes of {var}`specialArgs`

  Whereas option values can generally depend on other option values
  thanks to laziness, this does not apply to `imports`, which
  must be computed statically before anything else.

  For this reason, callers of the module system can provide `specialArgs`
  which are available during import resolution.

  For NixOS, `specialArgs` includes
  {var}`modulesPath`, which allows you to import
  extra modules from the nixpkgs package tree without having to
  somehow make the module aware of the location of the
  `nixpkgs` or NixOS directories.
  ```
  { modulesPath, ... }: {
    imports = [
      (modulesPath + "/profiles/minimal.nix")
    ];
  }
  ```

For NixOS, the default value for this option includes at least this argument:
- {var}`pkgs`: The nixpkgs package set according to
  the {option}`nixpkgs.pkgs` option.
_module.args.type: lazy attribute set of raw value

core.boot.encryptedDevice.description: Device to use for encrypted boot

core.fail2ban.banTimeIncrement.description: Whether to enable fail2ban ban time increment.

core.git.signingKey.description: The key to use for git signing

core.git.userEmail.description: The email to use for git commits

core.git.userName.description: The name to use for git commits

core.gpg.enable.description: Whether to enable GPG with SSH agent support.

core.gpg.pinentryPackage.description: Pinentry package to use for password entry

core.gpg.publicKeys.description: List of public GPG keys to import. Can be key data (strings) or file paths.

core.network.firewall.allowedTCPPortRang.description: List of TCP port ranges to allow

core.network.firewall.allowedTCPPorts.description: List of TCP ports to allow

core.network.firewall.allowedUDPPortRang.description: List of UDP port ranges to allow

core.network.firewall.allowedUDPPorts.description: List of UDP ports to allow

core.network.nm.extraPlugins.description: List of extra NetworkManager plugins to install

core.network.nm.unmanaged.description: List of network interfaces to not manage with NetworkManager

core.nix-helpers.nh.clean.dates.description: How often to run nh clean (systemd timer calendar).

core.nix-helpers.nh.clean.enable.description: Whether to enable periodic nh clean user (garbage collection).

core.nix-helpers.nh.clean.extraArgs.description: Extra arguments passed to nh clean.

core.nvf.spellcheck.additionalWords.description: Additional words to add to the spellcheck dictionary

core.nvf.spellcheck.languages.description: Languages to add to the spellcheck dictionary

core.polkit.allowUserPowerCommands.description: Whether to allow user to execute power commands

core.shell.packages.description:
Attrset of shell packages to install and add to pkgs.custom overlay (for compatibility across multiple shells).
Both string and attr values will be passed as arguments to writeShellApplicationCompletions
core.shell.packages.type: attribute set of (string or (attribute set) or package)

core.shell.qnixAliases.description: Whether to enable aliases for qnix-system

core.sops.age.generateKey.description: Whether to generate an age key automatically.

core.sops.age.keyFile.description: Path to the age key file. Defaults to /persist/home/<user>/.config/age/keys.txt if user is available.

core.sops.defaultSopsFile.description: Default Sops File to decrypt and use. Can be overridden per-secret with sopsFile.

core.sops.secrets.description: Secrets to decrypt and provision. Each secret maps to sops.secrets.*

core.sops.secrets.<name>.format.description: Format of the secret. 'binary' for raw bytes, 'yaml' for YAML parsing.

core.sops.secrets.<name>.group.description: Group of the decrypted secret file.

core.sops.secrets.<name>.key.description: Key name in the sops file. Defaults to the secret name.

core.sops.secrets.<name>.mode.description: File mode (permissions) for the decrypted secret file.

core.sops.secrets.<name>.neededBy.description: List of systemd units that need this secret.

core.sops.secrets.<name>.neededForUsers.description: Whether this secret is needed for user creation. Automatically set to true if referenced via passwordFromSops.

core.sops.secrets.<name>.owner.description: Owner of the decrypted secret file.

core.sops.secrets.<name>.path.description: Path where the decrypted secret will be placed. Defaults to /run/secrets/<name>.

core.sops.secrets.<name>.reloadUnits.description: List of systemd units to reload when this secret changes.

core.sops.secrets.<name>.restartUnits.description: List of systemd units to restart when this secret changes.

core.sops.secrets.<name>.sopsFile.description: Path to the sops file containing this secret. Defaults to defaultSopsFile.

core.sops.secrets.<name>.wantedBy.description: List of systemd units that want this secret.

core.ssh-server.port.description: Port to listen on for SSH connections

core.ssh-server.sshAgent.description: Whether to start the ssh agent

core.stylix.colorScheme.description: The color scheme to use, must be in base16scheme repo

core.stylix.colorSchemeOverrides.description: Override the color scheme with custom colors

core.stylix.solarizedColors.description: Colors converted from stylix base16 to solarized naming scheme (base03, base02, base01, base00, base0, base1, base2, base3, red, orange, yellow, green, cyan, blue, violet, magenta)

core.user.defaultExtraGroups.description: Groups to add to all users by default (e.g., [ "video" "audio" ])

core.user.root.password.description: The hashed password for the root user (mutually exclusive with passwordFromSops)

core.user.root.passwordFromSops.description: Name of the sops secret containing the root user's hashed password (mutually exclusive with password)

core.user.users.description: Users to create (attrset: username -> user config)

core.user.users.<name>.initialHashedPass.description: Initial hashed password (mutually exclusive with passwordFromSops)

core.user.users.<name>.isNormalUser.description: Whether this is a normal user (exactly one of isNormalUser or isSystemUser must be set)

core.user.users.<name>.isSystemUser.description: Whether this is a system user (exactly one of isNormalUser or isSystemUser must be set)

core.user.users.<name>.passwordFromSops.description: Name of the sops secret containing this user's hashed password (mutually exclusive with initialHashedPassword)

core.yubikey.autolock.description: Whether to enable autolock on yubikey removal.

core.yubikey.gui.description: Whether to enable yubikey management guis.

core.yubikey.login.description: Whether to enable login with yubikey.

core.yubikey.sudo.description: Whether to enable sudo with yubikey.

desktop.browser.brave.enable.description: Whether to enable Brave browser (annoying features disabled via policy).

desktop.displaymanager.sddm.enable.description: Whether to enable SDDM display manager.

desktop.displaymanager.sddm.theme.embedd.description: Embedded theme variant for the theme package

desktop.hyprdesktop.ags.configDir.description: Path to the AGS configuration (git checkout of qnix-ags or similar). If null, the qnix-ags flake's configDir is used.

desktop.hyprdesktop.ags.displays.large.description: Outputs that should use the wide AGS bar layout.

desktop.hyprdesktop.ags.displays.small.description: Outputs that should use the condensed AGS bar layout.

desktop.hyprdesktop.ags.extraPackages.description: Extra packages to use when overriding ags in dev shells or elsewhere. Note: hyprland, battery, notifd, tray, and wireplumber are always included.

desktop.hyprdesktop.hyprsuite.hyprland.s.description: Whether to enable set default animations.
Whether to enable set default keybinds.
Whether to enable set default special workspace settings.
Whether to enable set default window rules.

desktop.laptop-specifics.enable.description: Whether to enable laptop-specific services (Tuned, upower).

desktop.laptop-specifics.tuned.enable.description: Whether to enable TuneD for power/performance profiles (replaces power-profiles-daemon).

desktop.laptop-specifics.tuned.profile.description:
Default TuneD profile. quiet = custom profile stricter than powersave (CPU cap + power hints);
full-power = maximum throughput (throughput-performance).

desktop.laptop-specifics.tuned.quietMaxP.description:
When using the quiet profile, cap CPU at this percentage of max frequency (Intel P-State / AMD).
Lower = cooler/quieter, higher = more headroom (e.g. 50 = very quiet, 70 = balanced quiet).

desktop.laptop-specifics.upower.description: Whether to enable upower (battery/power info; required by TuneD when using PPD compatibility).

desktop.vscode.agentPanelSize.description: Default width (in pixels) for the Cursor agent panel/sidebar

desktop.vscode.enable.description: Whether to enable VS Code / Cursor.

desktop.vscode.package.description:
VS Code package to install via the Home Manager vscode module
(for example, pkgs.vscode, pkgs.vscodium or pkgs.cursor).
If not set, the default package will be pkgs.cursor.

desktop.waypaper.defaultWallpaper.description: Default wallpaper filename to use (relative to ~/Pictures/wallpaper)

desktop.waypaper.wallpaperSource.description: Source directory containing wallpapers to copy to ~/Pictures/wallpaper

persist.home.cache.directories.description: Directories to cache in home filesystem

persist.home.cache.files.description: Files to cache in home filesystem

persist.home.directories.description: Directories to persist in home filesystem

persist.home.files.description: Files to persist in home filesystem

persist.root.cache.directories.description: Directories to cache in root filesystem

persist.root.cache.files.description: Files to cache in root filesystem

persist.root.directories.description: Directories to persist in root filesystem

persist.root.files.description: Files to persist in root filesystem
